raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 1094 makes clear that affordable housing developers cannot be blocked from state funding in situations where cities and counties are noncompliant with housing element laws.",
    "The penalty imposed on affordable housing developers for jurisdictions missing compliance deadlines is counterproductive and hinders efforts to address the affordable housing shortage in California.",
    "Local governments must adopt Housing and Community Development-approved housing elements within a specific amount of time to be deemed in full compliance.",
    "Currently, 1% of localities in the Southern California Association of Governments have housing elements in compliance with state law.",
    "Senate Bill 1094 proposes sliding scale penalties for cities to incentivize compliance with housing element laws, thereby enabling the funding of affordable housing projects.",
    "The bill adjusts the 'pro-housing' designation law to ensure that its incentives apply only to programs in which local governments are the sole eligible applicants for funding.",
    "SB 1094 allows Regional Housing Needs Allocation (RHNA) credit to jurisdictions that create interim housing for people experiencing homelessness.",
    "Cities partnering with local home-sharing programs to create affordable units from existing housing stock will also receive RHNA credit under SB 1094.",
    "The changes proposed in SB 1094 provide further incentive for affordable housing acquisition by permitting local governments to receive RHNA credit for units converted from market-rate housing."
  ]
}
```
